text:
Tsudonke (ツドンケ, Tsudonke) is a supervisor in the Cha-R Family. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 380 

Appearance

Tsudonke has fair hair that is shaved on both sides. He has freckles on both cheeks.

Personality

Cha-R Family Head Brocco Li mentions that some of their younger associates are secret fans of the Phantom Troupe; Tsudonke appears to be among them. Upon meeting some members of the Troupe, he appears calm and collected, though inwardly, he is excited to ask them questions and get their autographs, but remains aware that it would set a bad example, and decides to keep an eye out for a chance to do so. 

Plot

Succession Contest arc

When Ken'i Wang brings three of the Spiders back to the Cha-R Family Office, he calls Tsudonke and three other men over to inform them that Phinks, Nobunaga, and Feitan are now joining the surveillance rotation, before ordering Tsudonke's group to guide them around. 

Tsudonke introduces himself as a supervisor to the Spiders and explains to Phinks that roughly 250 Cha-R Family members are on the boat. He explains that they could get food from the stockpile anytime, though they can also request a proper meal from the cafeteria on Tier 4. As he speaks, Tsudonke inwardly wonders if he could get some alone time with each Spider and request an autograph. 

Later, Tsudonke speaks with an old lady shopkeeper on Tier 3, who tells Tsudonke that gunshots were apparently heard in Area E's plaza, describing the situation with soldiers, the Xi-Yu, and civilians. As he hands her cash for an item, he asks her if she had been able to ask anyone from the military about the matter, but hears that there was a gag order in effect, and the shopkeeper speculates that a war was going to happen. She tells him that the foot soldiers on the lower tiers could be bribed for information, but would not have any information worth buying. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 392 

Tsudonke changes topics and asks her if she sold autograph boards. She asks if he is out of his mind and tells her that she did not have space for things like that, only stocking items like tobacco, alcohol, and drugs that customers pay ten times the retail price for, and adding that no shop on Tier 3, legitimate or not, would carry something like that. He thinks about how he'd pay one hundred times the price for it right now, and agrees with her statement that autograph boards were not something usually needed. He wonders if he should use something else, but decides that he would want to get the autograph done properly on a board, instead of whatever he had on hand. The shopkeeper tells him that he could try a dealer at a general store on Tier 1, sharing that although it would be expensive, dealers could bring in items from shore, and lists various methods of delivery. 

After thanking the old woman, Tsudonke regroups with several Cha-R members and tells himself that he needs to look out for a suspicious man over 190 cm (6'2"), saying that he has four days to find Hisoka so that he can ask for autograph paper as his reward. A Cha-R member tells Ken'i that someone clashed with the Xi-Yu in Area E's plaza, adding that Hinrigh was apparently involved. After saying that they will head there now, Ken'i addresses Tsudonke and his men, telling them to look for more information up to Area C if necessary and to report their findings to the Troupe back at the office. 

Later, Tsudonke and his men return to the Cha-R office, with Nobunaga opening the door for them. Hiding his excitement at Nobuanga's gesture, Tsudonke asks what happened to the other Cha-R members. Nobunaga reveals that they were all killed, leading them to Luini's decapitated and bisected corpse. With Tsudonke unable to believe the carnage, Nobunaga explains that they found the hitman who killed the guards at the warehouse, holding up his head and adding that they have decided to destroy the Heil-Ly Family. Feitan thanks them for their hospitality and says that the other door can wait. Phinks adds that while they plan to continue their search for Hisoka, any information on the Heil-Ly and the location of their boss will let them take care of that problem first. Tsudonke continues to hold in his excitement and tells himself that he has to get enough autograph paper for each Troupe member, hoping that Ken'i will help him access the general store. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 393 

Manga Appearances

Trivia

Intertextuality and References

Tsudonke could be a reference to Donketsu (ドンケツ), the name of a Yakuza-themed manga series by Ta-shi (たーし) and an anagram of Tsudonke (ツドンケ).
Within the Donketsu manga, the character "Rocket Masa" is insultingly called "donketsu", referring to a bottom-of-the-hierarchy member of the Yakuza. Like Tsudonke seems to be, Rocket Masa is near the bottom of his family's hierarchy.
